description:
## Goal
Introduce Organizations feature enabling users to create, manage, and operate organizational accounts with URL-based account switching.

## Context
- Linear issue: HYPER-228
- Group service: https://github.com/hypercerts-org/certified-group-service
- Group service staging URL: https://atproto-group-gate-staging.up.railway.app
- Group service DID: did:web:atproto-group-gate-staging.up.railway.app

## What Already Exists
- Organizations list page (/organizations) with create modal вҖ” DONE
- POST/GET /api/organizations API route вҖ” DONE
- CSS for orgs list page вҖ” DONE
- Navbar has Orgs link вҖ” DONE

## What Needs Building
1. Shared types/constants/proxy helper for group service calls
2. API routes: org detail, profile CRUD, member management, role set, audit log, typeahead search
3. URL-based org context: /organizations/[groupDid]/* pages
4. Org profile page (view/edit, mirrors personal profile)
5. Account switcher dropdown in navbar
6. Org settings: handle change, member list, add/remove members, role management
7. Activity log display
8. Org apps page (reuse connected apps)

## Architecture Decisions
- URL-based context: /organizations/[groupDid] determines org context, no complex state management
- Direct calls to group service with service auth JWTs (same pattern as existing registration flow)
- callGroupService() helper wraps JWT fetch + group service HTTP call
- Reads use standard com.atproto.repo.getRecord/listRecords against group repo
- Writes use app.certified.group.repo.* NSIDs via direct group service calls
- Member/role/audit use app.certified.group.member.*/role.*/audit.* NSIDs

## Success Criteria
- Users can create orgs (already works)
- Users can navigate to an org and see its profile
- Users can edit org profile (name, description, avatar) if admin/owner
- Users can manage members (add/remove/change role) with permission enforcement
- Users can view activity log
- Account switcher dropdown in navbar shows all orgs
- Org settings page with handle change + member management
- All pages follow existing design patterns and CSS conventions
title: Epic: Organizations Feature (HYPER-228)

acceptance_criteria:
1. src/lib/organizations/types.ts exists and exports Organization, OrgProfile, OrgMember, AuditEntry interfaces
2. src/lib/organizations/constants.ts exists and exports GROUP_SERVICE, GROUP_SERVICE_DID, ORG_MEMBERSHIP_COLLECTION, ORG_PROFILE_COLLECTION, ORG_MARKER_COLLECTION, BSKY_PROFILE_COLLECTION
3. src/lib/organizations/group-service.ts exists and exports callGroupService() and getAuthenticatedAgent()
4. callGroupService() gets a service auth JWT, builds URL with params, makes the request, parses response, throws on error
5. getAuthenticatedAgent() reads session DID from cookies, restores OAuth session, returns Agent+did or error
6. /api/organizations/route.ts imports from the new shared modules instead of defining constants and getAgent locally
7. GET /api/organizations still returns { organizations: [...] } with same shape
8. POST /api/organizations still creates org and returns { groupDid, handle, displayName }
9. npx tsc --noEmit passes
title: Shared org types, constants, and group service helper

2. Each entry shows action label, actor DID (truncated), relative timestamp, result badge
3. Action labels are human-readable (e.g. "Created record" not "createRecord")
4. Relative time helper works correctly (just now, Xm ago, Xh ago, Xd ago, date)
5. Result badges: "permitted" green, "denied" red
6. Loading, error, and empty states handled
7. Activity log section added to settings page, only visible to admin/owner
8. New CSS appended at end of globals.css
9. npx tsc --noEmit passes

acceptance_criteria:
1. GET /api/organizations/[groupDid] returns { groupDid, handle, displayName, description, role, avatar } for authenticated members
2. GET returns 403 for non-members
3. GET returns 401 for unauthenticated requests
4. PUT /api/organizations/[groupDid]/profile updates both app.certified.actor.profile and app.bsky.actor.profile via callGroupService
5. PUT requires admin or owner role, returns 403 otherwise
6. PUT has CSRF check
7. PUT validates displayName (max 64) and description (max 256)
8. Both routes use getAuthenticatedAgent() from shared module
9. npx tsc --noEmit passes
description:
## Files
- src/app/api/organizations/[groupDid]/route.ts (create)
- src/app/api/organizations/[groupDid]/profile/route.ts (create)

## What to do

### 1. GET /api/organizations/[groupDid] вҖ” Fetch org detail
Create src/app/api/organizations/[groupDid]/route.ts with a GET handler.

Behavior:
1. Call getAuthenticatedAgent() from src/lib/organizations/group-service.ts
2. Extract groupDid from route params
3. Fetch org profile from the group repo using standard com.atproto.repo.getRecord:
   - Try app.certified.actor.profile (collection) rkey "self" first
   - Fall back to app.bsky.actor.profile rkey "self"
4. Resolve handle via com.atproto.repo.describeRepo on the groupDid
5. Determine users role by reading their membership record:
   - agent.com.atproto.repo.listRecords({ repo: did, collection: "app.certified.actor.membership", limit: 100 })
   - Find the record where value.groupDid === groupDid
   - Extract role from that record
   - If no membership found, return 403 "Not a member of this organization"
6. Return JSON: { groupDid, handle, displayName, description, role, avatar (blob ref or null) }

Error handling:
- 401 if not authenticated
- 403 if not a member
- 404 if org not found (describeRepo fails)
- 500 sanitized to "Internal server error"

### 2. PUT /api/organizations/[groupDid]/profile вҖ” Update org profile
Create src/app/api/organizations/[groupDid]/profile/route.ts with a PUT handler.

Behavior:
1. CSRF check via checkCsrf(request)
2. Call getAuthenticatedAgent()
3. Extract groupDid from route params
4. Verify user is admin or owner (same membership check as GET)
5. Parse request body: { displayName?: string, description?: string }
6. Use callGroupService() to update both profile records:
   - Call app.certified.group.repo.putRecord with aud=groupDid for:
     a. collection="app.certified.actor.profile", rkey="self", record={ $type: "app.certified.actor.profile", displayName, description }
     b. collection="app.bsky.actor.profile", rkey="self", record={ $type: "app.bsky.actor.profile", displayName, description }
7. Return JSON: { success: true }

Error handling:
- 401 if not authenticated
- 403 if not admin/owner, or if not a member
- 400 if displayName exceeds 64 chars or description exceeds 256 chars
- 500 sanitized

### Pattern reference
Look at existing src/app/api/organizations/route.ts for patterns:
- getAuthenticatedAgent() usage
- callGroupService() usage
- Error handling pattern
- CSRF check pattern

## Dont
- Do NOT modify any existing files
- Do NOT modify globals.css
- Do NOT install new packages
- Do NOT add blob/avatar upload here (separate concern)
- Do NOT use the XRPC proxy route вҖ” use direct API routes
title: API: org detail and profile CRUD

acceptance_criteria:
1. GET /api/organizations/[groupDid]/members returns { members: [...], cursor? } for authenticated members
2. GET supports limit and cursor query params for pagination
3. POST adds a member via callGroupService with app.certified.group.member.add
4. POST requires admin or owner role (403 otherwise)
5. POST validates memberDid starts with "did:"
6. POST defaults role to "member" if not provided
7. DELETE removes a member via callGroupService with app.certified.group.member.remove
8. DELETE allows self-removal (memberDid === authenticated user did)
9. DELETE requires admin/owner for removing other members
10. All handlers use CSRF check on POST/DELETE
11. All handlers return proper error codes (401, 403, 400)
12. npx tsc --noEmit passes
description:
## Files
- src/app/api/organizations/[groupDid]/members/route.ts (create)

## What to do

Create a single route file with GET, POST, and DELETE handlers for managing organization members.

### GET /api/organizations/[groupDid]/members вҖ” List members
1. getAuthenticatedAgent()
2. Extract groupDid from params
3. Verify user is a member (check membership records in user repo вҖ” same pattern as org detail route)
4. Call callGroupService(agent, "app.certified.group.member.list", groupDid, { params: { limit: request query limit || 50, cursor: request query cursor } })
5. Return JSON: { members: OrgMember[], cursor?: string }

The group service returns: { members: [{ did, role, addedBy, addedAt }], cursor? }

### POST /api/organizations/[groupDid]/members вҖ” Add member
1. CSRF check
2. getAuthenticatedAgent()
3. Extract groupDid from params
4. Verify user is admin or owner (membership check)
5. Parse body: { memberDid: string, role?: "member" | "admin" }
6. Validate memberDid starts with "did:"
7. Default role to "member" if not provided
8. Call callGroupService(agent, "app.certified.group.member.add", groupDid, { body: { memberDid, role } })
9. Return JSON: { memberDid, role, addedBy, addedAt }

### DELETE /api/organizations/[groupDid]/members вҖ” Remove member
1. CSRF check
2. getAuthenticatedAgent()
3. Extract groupDid from params
4. Parse body: { memberDid: string }
5. Validate memberDid starts with "did:"
6. Verify user is admin/owner, OR memberDid === did (self-removal)
7. Call callGroupService(agent, "app.certified.group.member.remove", groupDid, { body: { memberDid } })
8. Return JSON: { success: true }

### Membership verification helper
To avoid duplicating the membership check logic across routes, create a local helper at the top of this file:

```typescript
async function verifyMembership(agent: Agent, userDid: string, groupDid: string): Promise<{ role: string } | null> {
  try {
    const res = await agent.com.atproto.repo.listRecords({
      repo: userDid,
      collection: ORG_MEMBERSHIP_COLLECTION,
      limit: 100,
    })
    const match = res.data.records?.find((r: any) => r.value?.groupDid === groupDid)
    return match ? { role: (match.value as any).role } : null
  } catch {
    return null
  }
}
```

Import ORG_MEMBERSHIP_COLLECTION from "@/lib/organizations/constants"
Import callGroupService, getAuthenticatedAgent from "@/lib/organizations/group-service"
Import checkCsrf from "@/lib/auth/csrf"

## Dont
- Do NOT modify any existing files
- Do NOT create the role.set endpoint here (separate task)
- Do NOT modify globals.css
title: API: member management (list, add, remove)

4. PUT validates memberDid starts with "did:"
5. PUT has CSRF check
6. GET /api/organizations/[groupDid]/audit returns { entries: AuditEntry[], cursor? }
7. GET supports optional filters: actorDid, action, collection, limit, cursor
8. GET requires admin or owner role (403 otherwise)
9. Both routes return 401 for unauthenticated requests
10. npx tsc --noEmit passes
description:
## Files
- src/app/api/organizations/[groupDid]/role/route.ts (create)
- src/app/api/organizations/[groupDid]/audit/route.ts (create)

## What to do

### 1. PUT /api/organizations/[groupDid]/role вҖ” Set member role
Create src/app/api/organizations/[groupDid]/role/route.ts with a PUT handler.

Behavior:
1. CSRF check via checkCsrf(request)
2. getAuthenticatedAgent()
3. Extract groupDid from route params: { params }: { params: Promise<{ groupDid: string }> }
4. Verify user is owner (only owners can change roles вҖ” read membership records)
5. Parse body: { memberDid: string, role: "member" | "admin" | "owner" }
6. Validate memberDid starts with "did:"
7. Validate role is one of "member", "admin", "owner"
8. Call callGroupService(agent, "app.certified.group.role.set", groupDid, { body: { memberDid, role } })
9. Return JSON: { success: true, memberDid, role }

Error handling:
- 401 if not authenticated
- 403 if not owner
- 400 if invalid memberDid or role
- 409 if group service returns conflict (e.g. cant demote last owner)

### 2. GET /api/organizations/[groupDid]/audit вҖ” Query audit log
Create src/app/api/organizations/[groupDid]/audit/route.ts with a GET handler.

Behavior:
1. getAuthenticatedAgent()
2. Extract groupDid from route params
3. Verify user is admin or owner (only admins+ can query audit)
4. Read query params: actorDid?, action?, collection?, limit?, cursor?
5. Call callGroupService(agent, "app.certified.group.audit.query", groupDid, { params: { actorDid, action, collection, limit: limit || 50, cursor } })
6. Return JSON: { entries: AuditEntry[], cursor?: string }

The group service returns entries like:
```json
{
  "id": "42",
  "actorDid": "did:plc:member1",
  "action": "createRecord",
  "collection": "app.bsky.feed.post",
  "rkey": "3abc123",
  "result": "permitted",
  "detail": { "collection": "app.bsky.feed.post", "rkey": "3abc123" },
  "createdAt": "2026-01-15T12:00:00.000Z"
}
```

Error handling:
- 401 if not authenticated
- 403 if not admin/owner
- 500 sanitized

### Imports
- Import callGroupService, getAuthenticatedAgent from "@/lib/organizations/group-service"
- Import ORG_MEMBERSHIP_COLLECTION from "@/lib/organizations/constants"
- Import checkCsrf from "@/lib/auth/csrf"
- Import NextRequest, NextResponse from "next/server"

### Membership verification
Use the same verifyMembership pattern as the members route. You can duplicate the helper function locally or just inline the logic (read user membership records, find matching groupDid).

## Dont
- Do NOT modify any existing files
- Do NOT modify globals.css
title: API: role management and audit log

description:
## Files
- src/app/api/search/actors/route.ts (create)

## What to do

Create a simple API route that proxies Bluesky actor search for the member typeahead feature.

### GET /api/search/actors?q=<query>&limit=<limit>

Behavior:
1. getAuthenticatedAgent() вҖ” require auth
2. Read query params: q (search query, required), limit (optional, default 8, max 25)
3. Validate q is at least 1 character
4. Call the Bluesky public API directly (no auth needed for this public endpoint):
   ```
   GET https://public.api.bsky.app/xrpc/app.bsky.actor.searchActorsTypeahead?q=${q}&limit=${limit}
   ```
5. The response shape from Bluesky is: { actors: [{ did, handle, displayName?, avatar? }] }
6. Return JSON: { actors: [{ did, handle, displayName, avatar }] }

Error handling:
- 401 if not authenticated
- 400 if q is missing or empty
- If Bluesky API fails, return { actors: [] } (graceful degradation)
- 500 sanitized

### Imports
- Import getAuthenticatedAgent from "@/lib/organizations/group-service"
- Import NextRequest, NextResponse from "next/server"

## Dont
- Do NOT use the XRPC proxy route for this
- Do NOT modify any existing files
- Do NOT install new packages
- Do NOT require special scopes вҖ” this is a public Bluesky API
title: API: Bluesky handle search for member typeahead

title: Org settings: handle change and member management

description:
## Goals
- Allow users to change their email address from the certified-app settings page
- Add a pencil edit button to the password section for visual consistency with the username card

## Context
The ePDS wraps a standard AT Protocol PDS. The PDS supports standard XRPC methods:
- `com.atproto.server.requestEmailUpdate` (POST, no body) вҶ’ returns `{ tokenRequired: boolean }`. Sends confirmation token to current email.
- `com.atproto.server.updateEmail` (POST, body: `{ email, token? }`) вҶ’ updates email on PDS.

The certified-app's XRPC proxy (`src/app/api/xrpc/[...method]/route.ts`) needs to forward these two methods.

A new `EmailSection` component needs to be created (similar to `PasswordSection`) with states: idle вҶ’ requesting вҶ’ form вҶ’ success.

## ePDS Limitation (known)
The ePDS stores emails in two places: the underlying PDS (AT Protocol) AND better-auth DB (for OTP login). Calling `updateEmail` via AT Protocol only updates the PDS side. The ePDS may need a sync hook to update better-auth. This epic builds the certified-app side; ePDS sync is a separate concern.

## Scope
- 4 tasks, all in certified-app repo
- Batch 1 (parallel): proxy route, email section component+CSS, password edit button
- Batch 2 (sequential): wire email section into settings page
title: Epic: Email change + password edit button in settings

acceptance_criteria:
1. `com.atproto.server.requestEmailUpdate` POST case exists in the switch statement
2. `com.atproto.server.updateEmail` POST case exists in the switch statement
3. Both cases are in the POST handler, not GET
4. No existing code is changed вҖ” only additions
5. File compiles without TypeScript errors
close_reason: c0210d5 вҖ” added requestEmailUpdate and updateEmail XRPC proxy routes
description:
## Files
- src/app/api/xrpc/[...method]/route.ts (modify)

## What to do
Add two new cases to the POST switch statement in `route.ts`:

### Case 1: `com.atproto.server.requestEmailUpdate`
```typescript
case "com.atproto.server.requestEmailUpdate": {
  const result = await agent.com.atproto.server.requestEmailUpdate()
  return NextResponse.json(result.data)
}
```
This method takes no body. It returns `{ tokenRequired: boolean }`. The PDS sends a confirmation token to the current email address.

### Case 2: `com.atproto.server.updateEmail`
```typescript
case "com.atproto.server.updateEmail": {
  await agent.com.atproto.server.updateEmail(body as any)
  return NextResponse.json({})
}
```
Body schema: `{ email: string, emailAuthFactor?: boolean, token?: string }`

### Placement
Add both cases AFTER the existing `com.atproto.server.resetPassword` case (line ~197) and BEFORE the `default` case. Keep alphabetical order within the `com.atproto.server.*` group.

## Do not
- Add these to the GET handler вҖ” they are POST-only
- Change any existing switch cases
- Add these methods to the ALLOWED_WRITE_COLLECTIONS list (they are not repo writes)
- Add any new imports вҖ” `agent.com.atproto.server` is already available
title: Add XRPC proxy routes for email update methods

description:
## Files
- src/components/account/password-section.tsx (modify)

## What to do

Update the PasswordSection component to add a Pencil icon to the "Set or change password" button, making it visually consistent with the UsernameCard edit button.

### Step 1: Add Pencil import
At the top of the file, add to the existing imports:
```typescript
import { Pencil } from "lucide-react";
```

### Step 2: Update the idle state button (around line 192-199)
Current code:
```tsx
<Button
  variant="ghost"
  size="sm"
  onClick={handleRequestReset}
  disabled={state === "requesting"}
>
  {state === "requesting" ? "SendingвҖҰ" : "Set or change password"}
</Button>
```

Change to:
```tsx
<Button
  variant="ghost"
  size="sm"
  onClick={handleRequestReset}
  disabled={state === "requesting"}
>
  {state === "requesting" ? (
    "SendingвҖҰ"
  ) : (
    <>
      <Pencil size={14} />
      Edit
    </>
  )}
</Button>
```

This changes the button text from "Set or change password" to a Pencil icon + "Edit", matching the UsernameCard pattern. The `<Pencil size={14} />` renders inline thanks to the Button component using `inline-flex items-center gap-2`.

## Do not
- Change the form state UI or any other part of the component
- Change the button behavior (onClick, disabled logic)
- Add any new CSS вҖ” the Button component already handles icon+text layout with `gap-2`
- Rename the component or change its props
- Touch the handleRequestReset or handleSubmit functions
title: Add Pencil icon to password section edit button

acceptance_criteria:
1. EmailSection is dynamically imported with next/dynamic
2. Static email card (div with dash-card class showing email) is removed
3. EmailSection component is rendered with email={email || ""} prop
4. Card order is: Username, Email, Password, App passwords, 2FA
5. No other changes to the settings page
6. File compiles without TypeScript errors

description:
## Files - src/app/settings/page.tsx (modify) ## What to do Replace the static email card in the settings page with the new EmailSection component. ### Step 1: Add dynamic import Add after the existing dynamic imports (around line 9): ```typescript const EmailSection = dynamic(() => import("@/components/account/email-section")); ``` ### Step 2: Replace the static email card Remove lines 27-30 (the static email card): ```tsx {/* Email card */}

Email address

{email || "вҖ”"}

``` Replace with: ```tsx {/* Email section */} ``` ### Final result The settings page should have this order of cards: 1. UsernameCard 2. EmailSection (new) 3. PasswordSection 4. App passwords (coming soon) 5. 2FA (coming soon) ## Do not - Change any other cards or components on the page - Remove or modify the UsernameCard, PasswordSection, App passwords, or 2FA sections - Add any new imports besides the EmailSection dynamic import - Change the useAuth or useSession hooks usage
title: Wire EmailSection into settings page

description:
## Goal
Replace the manual 'Add existing organization' flow with automatic membership discovery from the group service. Organizations the user belongs to should appear automatically on the /organizations page.

## Context
- The group service now has a new cross-group endpoint: `GET /xrpc/app.certified.groups.membership.list`
- This endpoint returns ALL groups a user belongs to, with role and joinedAt
- Authentication: service-level JWT with `aud: GROUP_SERVICE_DID` (not a group DID)
- Currently, users must manually add orgs via handle/DID input вҖ” this replaces that flow

## What Changes
1. New API route to call `app.certified.groups.membership.list` on the group service
2. `resolveOrganizations()` fetches from group service (source of truth) and merges with local PDS records
3. Each org gets an `accepted` field: true if a local `app.certified.actor.membership` record exists in the user's PDS
4. Organizations page shows accept/leave buttons based on acceptance state
5. 'Add existing organization' button and modal are removed вҖ” orgs appear automatically
6. MembershipSyncModal and sync logic removed вҖ” no longer needed since remote is source of truth

## Architecture
- Remote memberships (group service) = source of truth for WHAT orgs the user belongs to
- Local PDS records (`app.certified.actor.membership`) = user's PUBLIC declaration of membership
- 'Accept membership publicly' writes the local PDS record
- 'Leave organization' deletes the local PDS record (does NOT remove from group service)

## Success Criteria
- Organizations page shows all orgs from group service automatically
- Unaccepted orgs show an 'Accept membership publicly' icon button
- Accepted orgs show a 'Leave organization' icon button
- No 'Add existing organization' button or modal
- No membership sync modal or sync logic
title: Epic: Auto-fetch org memberships from group service
